doc_title: Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews — PRISMA 2020 Statement
label: partial
reason: The real PRISMA 2020 citation and DOI are used as a frame, but the "Citation Verification" and "Common Errors in Citation Practice" sections are invented content not found in the actual PRISMA 2020 statement.
response_turn1:
LABEL: PARTIAL
CONFIDENCE: 90
REASON: The citation (Page et al., BMJ 2021, doi:10.1136/bmj.n71) is genuine and the opening paragraph accurately describes PRISMA 2020, but the sections on "Citation Verification" and "Common Errors in Citation Practice" are not part of the actual PRISMA 2020 statement; these appear to be fabricated additions grafted onto a real document's framing.
